changelog:
Secret-scanner JSON-key hardening + githook-fossil cleanup (surfaced by the Domdhi.Crypto + video-speed-controller fleet security pass). PRIMARY: `secret-patterns.cjs` Secret/Password assignment regexes now tolerate a quoted key name before the separator — the JSON config shape (a double-quoted `api_key`/`password` key, the single most common place a real key lives) previously slipped BOTH the PreToolUse Write/Edit scanner and any pre-commit use because the regex required the key name to sit directly before the `:`/`=` separator (the closing quote intervened). Added an optional closing-quote class before the separator; the unquoted `key = ` / `key: ` forms still match and plain prose still does not (no new false positives). Found by Domdhi.Crypto's first /review:security audit — the CoinGecko key in the gitignored config.local.json was never exposed, but the scanner backstop had a blind spot exactly where that key lives. +2 regression tests. SECONDARY (doc-only): `tools/publish-manifest.json` dropped its stale claim that adopters scaffold `.githooks/` — the githook fallback was retired 2026-05-09 and is no longer scaffolded, propagated by template-updater (`ROOT_TEMPLATE_DIRS` is empty), or shipped by publish; all install paths verified inert. Fleet note: a dead, non-executable `.githooks/pre-commit` fossil (still pointing at the long-renamed `secret-scanner.js`) was removed from the video-speed-controller adopter — adopter-local cleanup, not a template change. Gate: 1919 tests pass.

--- v4.69 ---
/review:evolve-skills pins its eval/grading subagents to Sonnet. The Orchestration Rule now dispatches the with_skill/baseline eval *runs* + grading as `general-purpose` with an explicit `model: sonnet`, and a new What-NOT-to-Do guardrail forbids running them on Opus. `general-purpose` is already a Sonnet-tier agent by frontmatter, so this is a load-bearing make-explicit (not a tier change): it stops a future caller from silently escalating the mechanical eval fleet to Opus while keeping Opus on the main-agent diagnosis + SKILL.md authoring (the actual judgment). Markdown-only; no gate impact.

--- v4.68 ---
Dispatch-template commit discipline + guardrail amend relaxation (surfaced by agent/memory diagnostics on the Domdhi.Crypto adopter). PRIMARY: `run-todo.md` and `do.md` Path B dispatch prompt templates gained an explicit `DO NOT COMMIT / git add / git commit — the orchestrator owns commits` block. Root cause of 4 rogue commits by general-purpose/sonnet across Crypto Epics 13/14/16: the lead-owns-commits rule lived only in retro lore, so every hand-written dispatch re-dropped it; baked into the template (after the existing DO NOT TOUCH file-ownership line; inherited by run-todo's one-round fast-path) it can no longer be omitted. SECONDARY: removed the `git commit --amend` nudge from guardrail-rules.yaml — amending the last unpushed commit is routine and reflog-recoverable, and the nudge was pure friction on message fix-ups; force-push protection and the `git rebase` history-rewrite nudge are retained. Markdown + guardrail-YAML only; no gate impact. (Shipped as b386c28/a946f4d during the diagnostics session but landed after the 4.67 bump; this entry restores version↔content traceability before the fleet/public sync.)

--- v4.67 ---
/evolve Step 2a — intake staleness is coverage-based, not mtime-based (Domdhi.Crypto cycle-2→3 secondary finding). The 'refresh stale intake via /listen' test compared the newest intake's date against the most-recent-archive date, so a same-day intake that predated the entire cycle (Crypto: the only intake was same-day but from the Epic-11 era, before the cycle it was meant to inform) read as FRESH and the re-plan seeded on signals that missed the whole cycle. Step 2a now tests the intake against the cycle's COMMIT RANGE: establish the window (start = most recent _archive/cycle-* date, or first commit for cycle 1; end = HEAD), read the intake's own frontmatter `generated:` timestamp (fall back to mtime only if absent), and treat it as stale if generated before a meaningful share of the cycle's feature commits landed (`git log --since=<generated>` still shows substantive cycle work) — equivalently, an intake predating the cycle's first feature commit is stale regardless of date. Markdown-only; no gate impact.

--- v4.66 ---
/evolve Step 1 gate — reconcile path for done-but-untracked work (Domdhi.Crypto cycle-2→3 adopter finding). The close-out gate was binary: open `[ ]`/`[!]` stories → STOP or `--force`. But on an actively-worked repo the usual cause of open checkboxes is *trackers left stale after the work shipped* (Crypto: all of cycle 2 shipped + green at 308 tests, every epic had a completion commit, yet TODO_epic13 still had 35 unchecked boxes and _backlog.md showed all 14 stories ⬜). `--force` is WRONG there — it records shipped work as 'Unfinished, carried forward' and pollutes the next backlog; the agent had to reconcile by hand. The EV4 cross-check (v4.59) already DETECTS the drift but stopped one step short of resolving it. The gate is now three-way (reconcile ▸ stop ▸ force): on open stories, cross-check the open set against git/gate reality (completion commits + `docs/.output/telemetry/_latest-summary.json` overall PASS); ① if git corroborates the work shipped → AskUserQuestion to confirm, rewrite stale [ ]→[x] / ⬜→✅ only for git-corroborated stories, commit the reconciliation on its own, then archive a clean cycle (preferred, ranked above --force); ② if git does NOT corroborate → STOP (genuinely incomplete); ③ --force stays the explicit override for deliberately carrying genuinely-incomplete work forward. Reconcile is gated on git corroboration so it can never silently seal an incomplete cycle (not a back-door --force). Design-basis + Anti-Patterns updated to match. Markdown-only (instruction-level gate); no gate impact. References Crypto memory evolve-reconcile-tracker-drift-before-archive.

--- v4.65 ---
gate.js R1 e2e false-green fixes (two template-core bugs surfaced by the first downstream Playwright wiring of the R1 leg in video-speed-controller). BUG 1 (false-RED): parseTestOutput matched Jest/Vitest/dotnet/Go/Rust/pytest but NOT Playwright — Playwright prints each outcome on its OWN line with the duration in PARENS and no 'Tests:'/'total' keyword (`15 passed (14.4s)`), so a green suite parsed as total=0 and isZeroCollected flipped it to FAILED. Added an anchored per-line matcher (passed|failed|flaky|skipped|interrupted|did not run) that accumulates across lines (flaky→passed, interrupted→failed) and is anchored `^…$` so a numbered failure-detail line (`1) e2e/foo.spec.js …`) can't match. BUG 2 (false-GREEN, the root cause): loadConfig() returned an explicit gate.config.json early, so the R1 e2e-script auto-detection — which lived ONLY in the auto-detect branch — never ran for a project with an explicit config but no e2e key → config.e2e === undefined → the e2e leg silently SKIPped → PASS without ever running E2E (the exact false-green class R1 was built to kill, hitting every mature explicit-config project). Fix: the auto-detect body is extracted to autoDetectConfig() and loadConfig() now ALWAYS computes it, layering the explicit config on top (`{...detected, ...explicit}`) — explicit keys win, omitted keys (notably e2e) fall back to auto-detection instead of staying undefined. Purely template-core; no gate.config.json added to this repo (root package.json auto-detects). +6 tests (3 Playwright parse, 2 explicit-config-merge, anchoring guard). Gate: 1917 tests pass.

--- v4.64 ---
template-updater syncs the active root .gitignore. Previously `update` synced only the INERT .claude/templates/root/gitignore copy, so ignore-rule changes (e.g. v4.63 untracking docs/.output/memories/) never reached existing adopters — the active root .gitignore was only ever written by scaffold.js at setup time. `update` now performs the same managed-block merge scaffold uses (scaffold.applyManagedBlock, given a backward-compatible {dryRun} option) just before the version.json marker: idempotent — replaces an existing block in place, appends one if absent, and preserves the adopter's own rules outside the block. --dry-run previews the action (created/appended/replaced/unchanged) without writing; a no-op when the source ships no gitignore template. Discovered via a fleet audit where Domdhi.Platform / DMO / Doomstick carried stale .gitignores that exposed local-only docs/.output/memories/ + .claude/agent-memory/. +9 tests. Gate: 1894 tests pass.

--- v4.63 ---
Component-creator split. The `system-builder` meta-skill (badly named, covered all three component types in one file) is replaced by two focused per-type creator skills: `agent-creator` (agent template, frontmatter contract, model-tier guide, persona/soul conventions, agent-vs-skill gate) and `command-creator` (command template, namespacing, gate/commit/telemetry house-style, platform-primitive + parser-pattern rules). Skill creation now routes to the existing `skill-creator` (Anthropic's, untouched) + `skill-authoring`; the two genuinely-unique skill bits from system-builder (doc-producer/technique archetypes + skill wiring checklist) were absorbed into `skill-authoring`, and its broken `system-builder` xref fixed. `/create:component` rewired to route by type. Research-backed (Anthropic ships per-type creators — `/agents`, plugin-dev's agent-creator, skill-creator — never an umbrella; community's highest-traction repos use per-type specialists). `.md` commands + colon namespace deliberately retained (commands==skills functionally, but non-plugin skills flatten the namespace). Skill count 22→23. Gate: 1885 tests pass.

--- v4.62 ---
Command namespace reorg. `/review:sweep` → `/sweep` (promoted to base — it's the one-keystroke maintenance orchestrator). `/organize` → `/review:organize` and `/status` → `/review:status` (demoted into the review namespace — housekeeping + reporting utilities, not daily-flow). All cross-references and telemetry labels updated; `organize.cjs` hook / `status.js` core script / `status.html` output untouched (code paths unchanged — only the slash-command invocation moved). NOTE for downstream sync: template-updater is additive (never deletes), so after syncing this version each adopter will carry ORPHAN copies at the OLD paths (`.claude/commands/review/sweep.md`, `.claude/commands/organize.md`, `.claude/commands/status.md`) until they are removed by hand or via the reconcile step. Markdown-only; no gate impact.

--- v4.61 ---
/review:sweep remediation of v4.55–v4.60 (guardrail nudge tier + /evolve). SECURITY (A1): in the Bash guardrail (guardrail.cjs processEvent) the four-tier path-access check now runs BEFORE the nudge/confirm decision — previously `rm -rf .env # guardrail:confirm` escalated a zeroAccessPath (a should-be-HARD-block) to a user-approvable confirm; the path-tier hard block now wins and ignores the escalation marker. (A2) Fixed the PowerShell nudge regex: `/Remove-Item .+-Recurse .+-Force/` never matched the canonical single-space `Remove-Item foo -Recurse -Force` (the .+ required a char BETWEEN the flags) — now matches either flag order. (A3) git-rm exemption anchored to a real git INVOCATION via `(?<!(?:^|[\s;&|(])git )` so `legit rm` and `X=git rm` (a var-assignment prefix on a real rm) still nudge; only a standalone `git rm` is exempt. 3 new live-rules regression tests. /evolve data-loss + dry-run fixes: (B1) Step 3 archives each file individually with a per-file existence guard + asserts the archive is populated before regeneration — a bare `git mv TODO_epic*.md` glob that did not expand aborted the whole git mv atomically, after which Step 5 overwrote the un-archived backlog (data loss). (B2) Step 4 _cycle-summary.md write now skipped under --dry-run; (B8) Step 2a /listen guarded under --dry-run; (B9) telemetry exception noted. (B4) emoji-only completeness files now gate-block on non-done states. (B5) cycle number derived from max(existing N)+1, not raw dir count. (B3) cp snapshots per-file guarded. (B6) /todo epic-number branch requires the entire trimmed INPUT to be one integer token (no '12 factor app setup' misroute). code-review skill: added a 'verify moved/relocated patterns still match at the new home' check. Gate: 1882 tests pass.

--- v4.60 ---
Tertiary /evolve enhancement — master-index next-epic resolution. Empty `/todo` (no INPUT) now reads docs/TODO_{Project}.md, picks the next epic (prefers an in-progress epic, else the first not-done one in index order; if all done, says cycle complete → suggests /evolve), resolves it to its epic number, and hands off to the epic-number branch. Closes the lifecycle 'what now?' loop no command previously read the master index for: after /evolve Step 5.6 makes the lead epic runnable, subsequent epics are picked up automatically by a bare `/todo` — the loop self-continues without re-running /evolve. Implemented as an inference source in /todo's empty-INPUT chain (#2, after conversation context, before handoff/git fallbacks) rather than a new /next command, to avoid command-surface bloat. evolve.md Step 5.6 cross-references the loop. Markdown-only; gate: 1879 tests pass.

--- v4.59 ---
/evolve adopter-finding remediation (Domdhi.Crypto first real run). (1) Primary — /evolve now ends RUNNABLE, not just planned: new Step 5.6 materializes the lead dependency-root epic into an execution-ready TODO_epic*.md via /create:project-epics-todo + /todo (only the lead epic, just-in-time; the rest break down as reached). Default on; --no-todo opts out. The Step-7 Next: line now points at a TODO that actually exists instead of a /run-todo with no contract behind it. (2) Secondary — /todo is now epic-number aware: `/todo 12` (or epic-N/epicNN) reads Epic N's stories straight from _backlog.md as the brief instead of treating the number as literal task text; mirrors /create:project-epics-todo and is the unit Step 5.6 calls. (3) EV4 — /evolve Step 1 completeness gate now cross-checks the _backlog.md emoji Story-Index against the checkbox tally and flags disagreement (checkboxes stay authoritative; stale-emoji drift is surfaced, not silently trusted). (4) EV7 — doc-drift.js TODO_SKIP_DIRS now includes `_archive` (underscore) so a closed cycle's git-mv'd TODO_epic*.md under docs/todo/_archive/cycle-N/ is no longer false-flagged as a misplaced TODO; the prior set only had `.archive` (dot). Already shipped in v4.57: EV3 (memory store in evidence sweep) + EV5 (headless orchestration). Gate: 1879 tests pass.
